summary: Wu et al. identify outlier tokens in Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) and introduce Dual-Stage Registers (DSR) to reduce these artifacts and improve image generation quality across ImageNet and text-to-image tasks.

blocks.content: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers

source_span.quote: # Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers

blocks.content: We study outlier tokens in Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) for image generation.

source_span.quote: We study outlier tokens in Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) for image generation.

blocks.content: We show that this phenomenon appears in both the encoder and denoiser of modern Representation Autoencoder (RAE)-DiT pipelines: pretrained ViT encoders can produce outlier representations, and DiTs themselves can develop internal outlier tokens, especially in intermediate layers.

source_span.quote: We show that this phenomenon appears in both the encoder and denoiser of modern Representation Autoencoder (RAE)-DiT pipelines: pretrained ViT encoders can produce outlier representations, and DiTs themselves can develop internal outlier tokens, especially in intermediate layers.

blocks.content: Moreover, simply masking high-norm tokens does not improve performance, indicating that the problem is not only caused by a few extreme values, but is more closely related to corrupted local patch semantics.

source_span.quote: Moreover, simply masking high-norm tokens does not improve performance, indicating that the problem is not only caused by a few extreme values, but is more closely related to corrupted local patch semantics.

blocks.content: To address this issue, we introduce Dual-Stage Registers (DSR), a register-based intervention for both components: trained registers when available, recursive test-time registers otherwise, and diffusion registers for the denoiser.

source_span.quote: To address this issue, we introduce Dual-Stage Registers (DSR), a register-based intervention for both components: trained registers when available, recursive test-time registers otherwise, and diffusion registers for the denoiser.

blocks.content: Across ImageNet and large-scale text-to-image generation, these interventions consistently reduce outlier artifacts and improve generation quality.

source_span.quote: Across ImageNet and large-scale text-to-image generation, these interventions consistently reduce outlier artifacts and improve generation quality.

blocks.content: Our results highlight outlier-token control as an important ingredient in building stronger DiTs.

source_span.quote: Our results highlight outlier-token control as an important ingredient in building stronger DiTs.
